reference: Dr. Matthias Troyer, 'Building the Modern Quantum Architecture' lecture series (2025-2026), quantum.microsoft.com

lecture_series.title: Balancing the Cost of Utility-Scale Quantum Computing

divincenzo_criteria.description: DiVincenzo's 5+2 criteria for physical implementation of quantum computing  used as a hardware-realism overlay on maturity stages.
criteria: Scalable physical system with well-characterized qubits
Long coherence times relative to gate times
(Optional) Interconversion of stationary and flying qubits
divincenzo_criteria.relevance: Applied at Stage C/D to validate hardware-readiness assumptions for advantage claims.

troyer_cost_model.description: Part 6 of the lecture series (upcoming): balancing the cost of utility-scale quantum computing  when does the quantum advantage justify the engineering and economic cost?

external_knowledge_sources.name: Quantum Algorithm Zoo
external_knowledge_sources.description: 400+ quantum algorithms indexed by speedup class

external_knowledge_sources.name: Error Correction Zoo
external_knowledge_sources.description: Comprehensive encyclopedia of quantum and classical error-correcting codes. Covers stabilizer, CSS, surface, color, QLDPC, bosonic, and topological codes with code relationships and decoder references.

external_knowledge_sources.name: arxiv (cs.QC + quant-ph)
external_knowledge_sources.description: Daily ingestion of peer-reviewed quantum computing papers

industry_developments.title: New chip protects wireless biomedical devices from quantum attacks
industry_developments.summary: Ultra-efficient chip enables strong post-quantum cryptography on energy-constrained edge devices. Reinforces NIST PQC migration urgency.

industry_developments.title: World Quantum Day 2026  answering trending questions
industry_developments.summary: Google Doodle features the Bloch Sphere. Mission focus: long-term journey from experimental physics to error-corrected utility-scale systems.

industry_developments.title: Building superconducting and neutral atom quantum computers
industry_developments.summary: Google expands to dual-modality: superconducting (scales in circuit depth) + neutral atoms (scales in qubit count ~10k qubits). Led by Dr. Adam Kaufman at Boulder, CO.

industry_developments.title: Quantum frontiers may be closer than they appear
industry_developments.summary: Google sets 2029 timeline for post-quantum cryptography migration, implying CRQC (Cryptographically Relevant Quantum Computer) expected around end of decade.

industry_developments.title: New photonic device efficiently beams light into free space
industry_developments.summary: Light-emitting structures enable advanced displays, high-speed optical communications, and larger-scale quantum computers  relevant for photonic QC interconnects.

industry_developments.title: Efficient cooling method could enable chip-based trapped-ion quantum computers
industry_developments.summary: New cooling technique improves scalability of trapped-ion QC  essential step toward practical utility.

industry_developments.title: Quantum Echoes algorithm  verifiable quantum advantage
industry_developments.summary: First demonstration of verifiable quantum advantage on Willow chip. Real-world application milestone for quantum computing.

proven_speedup.label: Proven Speedup
proven_speedup.description: Algorithms with mathematically proven quantum speedup over the best classical algorithm. These are the strongest candidates for utility-scale quantum advantage.

problems.troyer_assessment: QAE provides provable quadratic speedup for amplitude estimation. However, the constant-factor overhead of error correction means utility requires problems with ε < 10⁻⁴ where the quadratic advantage overcomes QEC cost.
problems.utility_threshold: Requires ~10⁶ logical operations to beat classical; current toy instance has ~40 logical qubits

problems.troyer_assessment: Shor's algorithm is the canonical example of superpolynomial quantum speedup. Utility requires factoring 2048+ bit integers  our 4-bit toy instance demonstrates the algorithm but is far from utility scale.
problems.utility_threshold: RSA-2048 factoring requires ~4000 logical qubits and ~10¹⁰ T-gates

problems.speedup: Quadratic: O(√N) vs classical O(N), provably optimal (BBBV)
problems.troyer_assessment: Grover's quadratic speedup is provably optimal but only halves effective key length. NIST already recommends doubled key sizes (AES-256). The practical threat is from Shor, not Grover.
problems.utility_threshold: AES-128 search requires ~2⁶⁴ Grover iterations + millions of logical qubits for the cipher oracle

problems.speedup: Quadratic: O(√N) vs classical O(N), provably optimal
problems.troyer_assessment: Provably optimal quadratic speedup, but the QRAM assumption for data loading is critical. Without efficient QRAM, the speedup may be negated by data-access overhead.
problems.utility_threshold: Requires N > 10⁶ with efficient oracle; current 4-qubit instance is far below

problems.speedup: Exponential: O(polylog(N)) vs classical O(N·κ) for sparse systems
problems.troyer_assessment: HHL offers exponential speedup but with critical caveats: (1) requires efficient state preparation, (2) only provides quantum state output (readout bottleneck), (3) condition number κ must be polylog(N). Practical utility requires all three conditions simultaneously.
problems.utility_threshold: Utility requires N > 10⁶ with κ = O(polylog N) and efficient I/O

heuristic_potential.label: Heuristic / Unproven Advantage
heuristic_potential.description: Variational and approximate algorithms without proven speedup over classical. These rely on empirical performance that may or may not survive scaling. Troyer emphasizes these are the highest-risk category for utility claims.
heuristic_potential.note: Five problems originally in this category (01_hubbard, 02_catalysis, 07_drug_discovery, 14_materials_discovery, 17_nuclear_physics) were upgraded from VQE to QPE and reclassified to simulation_native. Their QPE implementations target naturally quantum Hamiltonians with exponential speedup.

problems.speedup: None proven; depth-1 QAOA has known classical simulations
problems.troyer_assessment: No proven quantum advantage for QAOA on MaxCut. The Goemans-Williamson algorithm achieves 0.878-approximation in polynomial time. QAOA at constant depth can be classically simulated. Higher-depth QAOA may offer advantage but this is unproven.
problems.archival_reason: At most quadratic, no proven advantage

problems.troyer_assessment: Classical protein folding has been revolutionized by AI (AlphaFold). QAOA for lattice protein models has no proven advantage. The quantum approach would need to handle realistic all-atom models to compete.

QML speedups depend on data encoding assumptions. If classical data must be loaded into quantum states, the loading cost often negates the kernel speedup. Proven advantages exist only for specific, contrived problem structures.
Same QAOA limitations as MaxCut. Classical scheduling algorithms are highly optimized for industrial use. No proven quantum advantage for combinatorial optimization at any depth.
Trajectory optimization is well-served by classical methods. QAOA for routing/scheduling has the same unproven-advantage limitations as other combinatorial optimization problems.

simulation_native.label: Quantum Simulation (Native Advantage Potential)
simulation_native.description: Problems where the quantum system being simulated IS quantum  Feynman's original motivation. These have the strongest theoretical case for utility but require fault-tolerant hardware. Includes problems upgraded from VQE to QPE that target naturally quantum Hamiltonians.

problems.troyer_assessment: Upgraded to QPE targeting 2D Hubbard Hamiltonians  naturally quantum systems where tensor networks fail. QPE provides exponential speedup for strongly-correlated 2D systems. Our 2-site instance is pedagogical; utility requires >50-site 2D lattices beyond DMRG reach.
problems.upgrade_note: VQE→QPE (April 2026). Reclassified from heuristic_potential to simulation_native.

problems.troyer_assessment: Upgraded to QPE for molecular Hamiltonians. Troyer identifies quantum chemistry as the #1 application for quantum computing. QPE provides exponential speedup for molecules with >50 orbitals where FCI is intractable. Current H₂ instance is pedagogical.
problems.upgrade_note: VQE→QPE (April 2026). Reclassified from heuristic_potential to simulation_native.

problems.troyer_assessment: Financial QAE inherits the quadratic speedup of amplitude estimation. Utility requires portfolio sizes where the QEC overhead is justified by the quadratic speedup  likely N > 10⁴ assets with complex correlation structures.

Upgraded to QPE for molecular binding energy Hamiltonians. Quantum advantage in drug discovery requires simulating transition states of large molecules (>100 atoms) with strong electron correlation. QPE provides the exponential speedup path.
problems.upgrade_note: VQE→QPE (April 2026). Reclassified from heuristic_potential to simulation_native.

problems.troyer_assessment: Climate PDEs are well-suited to HHL if the state preparation and readout problems can be solved. The exponential speedup applies to the linear algebra core but practical utility requires end-to-end advantage including I/O.
Upgraded to QPE for strongly-correlated materials (e.g., high-Tc superconductors) where DFT fails. QPE provides exponential speedup for Hamiltonian simulation of correlated electron systems.
problems.upgrade_note: VQE→QPE (April 2026). Reclassified from heuristic_potential to simulation_native.

problems.troyer_assessment: QEC is enabling infrastructure, not an application. The repetition code demonstrates error detection but is not a path to utility. Surface codes and beyond are needed for fault-tolerant computation. See errorcorrectionzoo.org for comprehensive code taxonomy.

Upgraded to QPE for nuclear EFT Hamiltonians. Quantum advantage requires many-body nuclear systems with >20 nucleons where shell model truncation becomes severe. QPE provides the exponential speedup path for naturally quantum nuclear forces.
problems.upgrade_note: VQE→QPE (April 2026). Reclassified from heuristic_potential to simulation_native.

problems.troyer_assessment: Exciton transport in photosynthetic systems is inherently quantum. Simulating open quantum systems is where quantum computers have the strongest natural advantage  the system IS quantum.

problems.classical_best: Lattice QCD (Euclidean, limited to static properties)
problems.troyer_assessment: Lattice gauge theory is arguably the strongest utility candidate. Classical lattice QCD cannot simulate real-time dynamics due to the sign problem. Quantum simulation of gauge theories could enable predictions impossible classically  but requires millions of logical qubits for realistic lattice sizes.

summary.key_insight: 5/20 problems have proven quantum speedups (QAE, Shor, Grover×2, HHL). 5/20 rely on heuristic algorithms (QAOA×3, Swap Test, QAOA) with no proven advantage  all archived. 10/20 are quantum simulation problems (including 5 upgraded from VQE→QPE) with the strongest theoretical case for utility. 9 problems are active (passing all 5 Troyer filters); 11 archived. Per Troyer's framework, honest assessment is essential for credible quantum advantage claims.
summary.divincenzo_note: DiVincenzo's criteria (scalable qubits, initialization, coherence, universal gates, measurement) are applied as a hardware-realism overlay at Stage C/D. Current quantum hardware challenges  limited qubit counts, short coherence times, high error rates  map directly to DiVincenzo gaps that must close before utility-scale advantage is achievable.
